text:
There once was a young squirrel named Oliver who lived in a cozy oak tree near a garden bursting with colors. Every day, Oliver looked forward to the morning when he could explore the human world below and pick the tastiest berries. One day, while poking out his nose among pet ferns, he discovered a peculiar seed.

It wasn't like anything else Oliver had ever seen—I'm not sure why—I suspect something magical. Perhaps this seed was left behind by a mischievous fairy. That evening, intrigued, Oliver took it back home.

Oliver planted it in the dirt under his favorite oak. Days turned into weeks, and something mysterious began to happen. The seed began to grow, not into a bush or vine like any ordinary seed, but into a little owl. Oliver's heart swelled.

Every day, the owl's wise eyes would watch over him. Oliver taught it to fly around the garden, teaching it how to pick berries expertly, never causing bother. Eventually
